annotations.label: Product (multiplication result)

anchor.snippet: a product is the result of multiplication

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the core `Mul`/`HMul` typeclass with bundled structures `Semigroup`/`Monoid`/`CommMonoid` providing the multiplication operation; there is no standalone definition of 'product as result of multiplication'.

annotations.label: Multiple

anchor.snippet: When one factor is an integer , the product is called a multiple

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Dvd.dvd` and integer scalar multiplication (`zsmul`) capture 'multiple of', but there is no named definition specifically for 'multiple'.

annotations.label: Commutative law of multiplication

anchor.snippet: The order in which real or complex numbers are multiplied has no bearing on the product

annotations.note: `mul_comm` states `a * b = b * a` for `CommMagma`/`CommMonoid`, instantiating to real and complex numbers.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of arithmetic

anchor.snippet: The fundamental theorem of arithmetic states that every composite number is a product of prime numbers

annotations.note: `Nat.prod_primeFactorsList` expresses every positive natural as the product of its prime factors, and `UniqueFactorizationMonoid` provides the general statement.

annotations.label: Product in linear equation

anchor.snippet: in the linear equation

annotations.note: Illustrative example of multiplication; no specific Mathlib declaration corresponds to this.

annotations.label: Product of a sequence (Π notation)

anchor.snippet: The product operator for the product of a sequence is denoted by the capital Greek letter pi

annotations.note: `Finset.prod` with `∏` notation formalizes the product of a sequence over a finset.

anchors.snippet: The product operator for the product of a sequence is denoted by the capital Greek letter pi

annotations.label: Empty product

anchor.snippet: the product of no factors at all is known as the empty product , and is equal to 1

annotations.note: `Finset.prod_empty` states `∏ x ∈ ∅, f x = 1`, exactly the empty-product convention.

annotations.label: Addition of residue classes

anchor.snippet: Residue classes in the rings

annotations.note: `ZMod n` formalizes the ring of residue classes of integers mod n with its ring structure (including multiplication).

annotations.label: Convolution of two functions

anchor.snippet: Two functions from the reals to itself can be multiplied in another way, called the convolution

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.convolution` formalizes the convolution of two functions with respect to a measure.

annotations.label: Convolution under Fourier transform

anchor.snippet: Under the Fourier transform , convolution becomes point-wise function multiplication

annotations.note: `Real.fourier_mul_convolution_eq` (and `SchwartzMap.fourier_convolution`) state the Fourier transform converts convolution into pointwise multiplication.

annotations.label: Product of two polynomials

anchor.snippet: The product of two polynomials is given by the following

annotations.note: `Polynomial` carries a `Semiring`/`CommRing` instance and `Polynomial.mul_def` defines polynomial multiplication via finitely supported convolution of coefficients.

annotations.label: Scalar multiplication

anchor.snippet: one can form the product of any scalar with any vector

annotations.note: `SMul`/`HSMul` provide the scalar-multiplication operation, and `Module` bundles it with the vector-space axioms.

annotations.label: Scalar product

anchor.snippet: A scalar product is a bi-linear map

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace` formalizes a bilinear (sesquilinear over ℂ) inner product on a vector space.

annotations.label: Norm from scalar product

anchor.snippet: From the scalar product, one can define a norm by letting

annotations.note: `norm_eq_sqrt_re_inner` (and `InnerProductSpace.toNormedAddCommGroup`) build the norm `‖x‖ = sqrt ⟨x,x⟩` from the inner product.

annotations.label: Angle between two vectors

anchor.snippet: The scalar product also allows one to define an angle between two vectors

annotations.note: `InnerProductGeometry.angle` defines the unoriented angle via `arccos` of the normalized inner product.

annotations.label: Standard dot product in Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: the standard scalar product (called the dot product ) is given by

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace.inner_eq_star_dotProduct` (with `Matrix.dotProduct`) gives the standard sum-of-products dot product on `EuclideanSpace`.

annotations.label: Cross product in 3-dimensional space

anchor.snippet: The cross product of two vectors in 3-dimensions is a vector perpendicular to the two factors

annotations.note: `crossProduct : (Fin 3 → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin 3 → R) →ₗ[R] Fin 3 → R` formalizes the 3-D cross product.

annotations.label: Cross product as formal determinant

anchor.snippet: The cross product can also be expressed as the formal

annotations.note: `triple_product_eq_det` states `u ⬝ᵥ (v ⨯₃ w) = det ![u,v,w]`, the determinant expression for the cross/triple product.

annotations.label: Linear mapping

anchor.snippet: A linear mapping can be defined as a function f between two vector spaces V and W

annotations.note: `LinearMap` is the bundled structure of additive maps preserving scalar multiplication between modules/vector spaces.

annotations.label: Composition of linear mappings

anchor.snippet: Now we consider the composition of two linear mappings between finite dimensional vector spaces

annotations.note: `LinearMap.comp` defines the composition of two linear maps, yielding another linear map.

annotations.label: Product of two matrices

anchor.snippet: their product is a matrix

annotations.note: Matrix multiplication is defined via the `HMul`/`Mul` instances on `Matrix m n α` in `Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Mul`.

annotations.label: Matrix product as composition of linear functions

anchor.section: Composition of linear functions as matrix product
anchor.snippet: the matrix product is the description in coordinates of the composition of linear functions

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrix_comp` proves that the matrix of a composition of linear maps equals the matrix product of their individual matrices.

annotations.label: Tensor product of vector spaces

anchor.snippet: the tensor product of them can be defined as a (2,0)-tensor satisfying

annotations.note: `TensorProduct R M N` formalizes the tensor product of two modules/vector spaces with its universal `lift` property.

annotations.label: Monoidal category as class with tensor product

anchor.section: The class of all objects with a tensor product
anchor.snippet: a monoidal category is the class of all things (of a given type ) that have a tensor product

annotations.note: `MonoidalCategory` formalizes a category equipped with a tensor product, unit, associator and unitor isomorphisms.

annotations.label: Cartesian product of sets

anchor.snippet: the Cartesian product A × B is the set of all ordered pairs

annotations.note: `Set.prod` (notation `s ×ˢ t`) defines the Cartesian product of two sets as the set of ordered pairs.

annotations.label: Cartesian category

anchor.snippet: The class of all things (of a given type ) that have Cartesian products is called a Cartesian category

annotations.note: `CartesianMonoidalCategory` (related: `ChosenFiniteProducts`) formalizes categories whose monoidal structure is given by binary products and a terminal object.

annotations.label: Empty product equals identity

anchor.snippet: The empty product on numbers and most algebraic structures has the value of 1

annotations.note: `Finset.prod_empty : ∏ x ∈ ∅, f x = 1` codifies the empty-product-equals-one convention.

annotations.label: Product integral

anchor.snippet: A function's product integral

annotations.note: Searches for `productIntegral` and 'product integral' found no matches in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex multiplication

anchor.snippet: Complex multiplication , a theory of elliptic curves

annotations.note: Searches for `ComplexMultiplication`/CM theory of elliptic curves found no matches in Mathlib.
